title: viola canina subsp. schultzii
intro: Viola canina subsp. schultzii, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a subspecies of the Heath Dog Violet, a perennial plant in the violet family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in vitamin C and has been used to support immune function, improve skin health, and reduce the severity of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Wood Sorrel can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
